definition: Become less intense or widespread.
A departure from what is normal, usual, or expected.

definition: Regard with disgust and hatred.

definition: Extremely unpleasant and humiliating.

definition: Solemnly renounce (a belief, cause, or claim).

definition: Repeal or do away with (a law, right, or formal agreement).

definition: Difficult to understand; obscure.

definition: Agree to a demand, request, or treaty.

definition: An award or privilege granted as a special honor.

definition: Growth or increase by the gradual accumulation of additional layers or matter.

definition: Sharp and forthright (especially of a comment or style of speaking).
Accept something reluctantly but without protest.
Bitterness or ill feeling.

definition: The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions.
Warn or reprimand someone firmly.

definition: Clever or skillful in using the hands or mind.

definition: Excessive admiration or praise.

definition: Render (something) poorer in quality by adding another substance.

definition: Preventing success or development; harmful; unfavorable.

definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.

definition: Friendly, good-natured, or easy to talk to.
(especially of a group or area) having a great deal of money; wealthy.

definition: Increase the power, status, or wealth of.

definition: Brisk and cheerful readiness.

definition: Make (suffering, deficiency, or a problem) less severe.

definition: Not friendly or forthcoming; cool and distant.

definition: Showing a disinterested and selfless concern for the well-being of others.

definition: Combine or unite to form one organization or structure.

definition: Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something or someone.

definition: Make (something bad or unsatisfactory) better.

definition: (of a person) open and responsive to suggestion; easily persuaded or controlled.

definition: Having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner.

definition: Without a clearly defined shape or form.
Belonging to a period other than that being portrayed.
Something or someone that one vehemently dislikes.

definition: Append or add as an extra or subordinate part, especially to a document.

definition: Declare invalid (an official agreement, decision, or result).

definition: Something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected.

definition: Active hostility or opposition.

definition: Of or belonging to the time before the biblical Flood; ridiculously old-fashioned.

definition: A published collection of poems or other pieces of writing.
A deep-seated feeling of dislike; aversion.
Old-fashioned or outdated.

definition: Of, relating to, or denoting substances that prevent the growth of disease-causing microorganisms.

definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern.

definition: A person or thing that is the direct opposite of someone or something else.

definition: (of a story or statement) of doubtful authenticity, although widely circulated as being true.

definition: Pacify or placate (someone) by acceding to their demands.

definition: Assess the value or quality of.
Arrest (someone) for a crime.

definition: Approval or praise.

definition: Understood by few; mysterious or secret.

definition: Very typical of a certain kind of person or thing.

definition: Enthusiastic or passionate.

definition: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring.

definition: Take or claim (something) for oneself without justification.

definition: Characterized by or suggesting the practice of severe self-discipline and abstention from all forms of indulgence, typically for religious reasons.

definition: Attribute something to (a cause).

definition: An attack on the reputation or integrity of someone or something.

definition: Make a concerted or violent attack on.

definition: Conducive to success; favorable.

definition: Severe or strict in manner, attitude, or appearance.

definition: Of or relating to a ruler who has absolute power.

definition: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.

definition: A cause of great distress or annoyance.

definition: Reluctant to draw attention to oneself; shy.

definition: Charm or enchant (someone), sometimes in a deceptive way.

definition: A huge or monstrous creature; something enormous.

definition: (of an appearance) fail to give a true notion or impression of (something); disguise or contradict.

definition: Hostile and aggressive.
Well meaning and kindly.

definition: Deprived of or lacking something, especially a nonmaterial asset.

definition: A small mark or flaw which spoils the appearance of something.

definition: A thing that spoils or damages something.

definition: Of or characteristic of the middle class, typically with reference to its perceived materialistic values or conventional attitudes.

definition: Abrupt or offhand in speech or manner.

definition: The making of false and defamatory statements about someone in order to damage their reputation; slander.

definition: Cease to resist an opponent or an unwelcome demand; surrender.

definition: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior.

definition: Attract and hold the interest and attention of; charm.

definition: Drink plentiful amounts of alcohol and enjoy oneself with others in a noisy, lively way.

definition: Complain or find fault continually, typically about trivial matters.

definition: A meeting of the members of a legislative body who are members of a particular political party, to select candidates or decide policy.

definition: Jump or dance around excitedly.

definition: Express severe disapproval of (someone or something), typically in a formal statement.

definition: (of a person) calm, dependable, and showing little emotion or animation.

Cause to lose enthusiasm and initiative as a result of a tedious or restrictive routine.
Make (someone) unable to think or feel properly.
Bring under domination or control, especially by conquest.
Of such excellence, grandeur, or beauty as to inspire great admiration and awe.
Ready to conform to the authority or will of others; meekly obedient or passive.
Lower in rank or position.
Prepared to obey others unquestioningly.
Become less intense, violent, or severe.
Provide evidence to support or prove the truth of.
Deceit used in order to achieve one's goal.
(especially of a change or distinction) so delicate or precise as to be difficult to analyze or describe.
Seeking or intended to subvert an established system or institution.
(especially of something written or spoken) briefly and clearly expressed.
Fail to resist pressure, temptation, or some other negative force.
Bad-tempered and sulky; gloomy.
Damage the purity or integrity of; defile.
Splendid and expensive-looking.
Behaving or looking as though one thinks one is superior to others.
Existing or occurring at or on the surface.
Unnecessary, especially through being more than enough.
An excessive amount of something.
Suppose that something is true without having evidence to confirm it.
example: I surmise that he is busy.

definition: Kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of.
A substitute, especially a person deputizing for another in a specific role or office.
Likely or liable to be influenced or harmed by a particular thing.
A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important in order to gain advantage.
example: He was a sycophant who always agreed with the boss.

definition: Involving interaction between two different organisms living in close physical association.
Understood or implied without being stated.
(of a person) reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.
Relating to or along a tangent.
Perceptible by touch.
Equivalent in seriousness to; virtually the same as.
example: The news is tantamount to a declaration of war.

definition: Too long, slow, or dull; tiresome or monotonous.
Excessive confidence or boldness; audacity.
example: He had the temerity to ask.

definition: Abstinence from alcoholic drink.
Able to be maintained or defended against attack or objection.
Tending to keep a firm hold of something; clinging or adhering closely.
Not certain or fixed; provisional.
Very weak or slight.
Sparing in the use of words; abrupt.
(of a person or their behavior) using money and other resources carefully and not wastefully.
Showing or suffering from nervousness, fear, or a lack of confidence.
A long, angry speech of criticism or accusation.
A person who behaves obsequiously to someone important.
example: He was a toady who always praised the boss.

definition: A book, especially a large, heavy, scholarly one.
Mentally or physically inactive; lethargic.
Full of twists and turns.
(of a person or animal) easy to control or influence.
Free from disturbance; calm.
Infringe or go beyond the bounds of (a moral principle or other established standard of behavior).
Lasting only for a short time; impermanent.
(of a substance) allowing light, but not detailed images, to pass through; semitransparent.
Change in form, nature, or substance.
example: The lead was transmuted into gold.

definition: A false, absurd, or distorted representation of something.
Guilty of or involving betrayal or deception.
Vigorous or incisive in expression or style.
A feeling of fear or agitation about something that may happen.
(of a remark, opinion, or idea) overused and consequently of little import; lacking originality or freshness.
Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant.
Shorten (something) by cutting off the top or the end.
Swollen and distended or congested.
Depravity; wickedness.
Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
Offense or annoyance.
example: She took umbrage at the remark.

definition: Strange or mysterious, especially in an unsettling way.
(of a person) excessively or ingratiatingly flattering; oily.
Move with a smooth wavelike motion.
example: The grass undulated in the breeze.

definition: Find fault with (someone); scold.
Take (a position of power or importance) illegally or by force.
Designed to be useful or practical rather than attractive.
An imagined place or state of things in which everything is perfect.
Alternate or waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.
example: He vacillated between the two choices.

definition: Having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence; mindless.
Offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging.
Exhibiting different colors, especially as irregular patches or streaks.
Showing strong feeling; forceful, passionate, or intense.
A thin decorative covering of fine wood applied to a coarser wood or other material.
Accorded a great deal of respect, especially because of age, wisdom, or character.
Regard with great respect; revere.
example: He is venerated by his followers.

definition: Conformity to facts; accuracy.
Using or expressed in more words than are needed.
(of countryside) green with grass or other rich vegetation.
A trace of something that is disappearing or no longer exists.
example: The vestiges of the old city.

definition: Make (someone) feel annoyed, frustrated, or worried, especially with trivial matters.
Experienced in the imagination through the feelings or actions of another person.
A change of circumstances or fortune, typically one that is unwelcome or unpleasant.
Keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties.
Speak or write about in an abusively disparaging manner.
example: He was vilified by the press.

definition: Clear (someone) of blame or suspicion.
Having or showing a strong or unreasoning desire for revenge.
A person highly skilled in music or another artistic pursuit.
(of a disease or poison) extremely severe or harmful in its effects.
Having a thick, sticky consistency between solid and liquid; having a high viscosity.
Filled with bitter criticism or malice.
Blame or insult (someone) in strong or violent language.
(especially of a woman) attractively lively and animated.
A strong feeling of suitability for a particular career or occupation.
(especially of a person or speech) vehement or clamorous.
Not valid or legally binding.
The faculty or power of using one's will.
(of a person) talking fluently, readily, or incessantly.
(of a woman) curvaceous and sexually attractive.
Wanting or devouring great quantities of food.
(of the moon) have a progressively smaller part of its visible surface illuminated, so that it appears to decrease in size.
(of a cruel or violent action) deliberate and unprovoked.

A document issued by a legal or government official authorizing the police or some other body to make an arrest, search premises, or carry out some other action relating to the administration of justice.
Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems.
Skilled at gaining an advantage, especially deceitfully.
Attractive or appealing in appearance or character.
Having or showing a feeling of vague or regretful longing.
Shriveled or wrinkled with age.
Extreme anger (chiefly used for humorous or rhetorical effect).
Having or showing zeal.
The time at which something is most powerful or successful.
